evals.name: happy-path-account-with-ratified-ocv
evals.prompt: Run outcome tracing for Acme Corp.
evals.context: HubSpot connected. Acme Corp closed 90 days ago. OCV catalog present with OCV-001 (Ratified) linked to the deal. OCV-001 trigger condition matches account situation. CS platform connected: health_score=72, product_usage_trend=stable.
evals.expected_output: Skill produces a single-account longitudinal trace. Confirms catalog completeness (all 3 checks pass). Assesses rubric level using OCV-001 evidence criteria and CS platform signals. Labels data sources. Applies G5 and G6.

assertions.text: Output contains a catalog completeness section with results for all three checks: OCV entry referenced, trigger condition match, measurement source accessible
Output contains a current rubric level assessment (L0, L1, L2, or L3) with the OCV entry ID cited
Output applies G6: includes a data-as-of timestamp on CRM and CS platform data
Output applies G5: includes language assigning CS ownership of the response plan
Output includes a next checkpoint date derived from the OCV verification milestone
Output applies G8: does not use any Draft OCV entries in the assessment

evals.name: degraded-no-ocv-catalog
evals.context: HubSpot connected. Buildco closed 45 days ago. No OCV catalog configured (ocv_catalog_path absent from company profile). CS platform connected.
evals.expected_output: Skill detects absent OCV catalog, switches to structural fallback mode, runs Tier 1 structural risk signals only (discount level, cycle length, stakeholder count), and explicitly labels the output with the degraded confidence declaration.

assertions.text: Output explicitly declares OCV catalog absent using the canonical label: '[Outcome data: OCV reference absent — structural signals only — Confidence: Low]' or equivalent
Output does NOT produce a rubric level assessment (L0–L3) — this requires the OCV catalog
Output produces a structural risk assessment using available deal attributes (discount, cycle, stakeholder count)
Output does not proceed silently — the degraded state is declared before any analysis is shown

evals.name: trigger-condition-mismatch
evals.prompt: Check outcome tracing for Zeta Inc.
evals.context: HubSpot connected. Zeta Inc closed 60 days ago. OCV-002 (Ratified) is linked to the deal. OCV-002 trigger condition: 'When a mid-market SaaS company is losing revenue to manual billing errors'. Zeta Inc is an enterprise manufacturing company — trigger condition does NOT match.
evals.expected_output: Skill passes Check 1 (OCV entry present), fails Check 2 (trigger mismatch), flags this as 'Sold to wrong problem', surfaces it to CS and Sales manager, but continues with the available analysis rather than stopping entirely.

assertions.text: Output flags a trigger condition mismatch for OCV-002 — the account situation does not match the OCV trigger
Output uses language equivalent to 'Sold to wrong problem' or 'trigger condition not present in account context'
Output applies G7: names who should be notified of the mismatch (CS and Sales manager) and through which channel
Output applies G6: data-as-of timestamp present

evals.name: cohort-view-l0-pattern-detection
evals.prompt: Run a cohort outcome trace for all Enterprise accounts closed in Q1.
evals.context: HubSpot connected. 12 Enterprise accounts closed in Q1. OCV catalog present with OCV-003 (Ratified) on all 12 deals. At the 90-day checkpoint: 6 accounts at L0, 4 at L1, 2 at L2. L0 rate = 50% (threshold: >40%).
evals.expected_output: Skill produces cohort view with L0/L1/L2/L3 breakdown per OCV entry. Fires L0 pattern signal because >40% at L0 past 90-day checkpoint. Surfaces common attributes of L0 accounts. Does not name individual accounts in the pattern signal.

assertions.text: Output contains a cohort rubric level table showing L0/L1/L2/L3 counts and percentages for OCV-003
Output fires the L0 pattern signal because L0 rate (50%) exceeds the 40% threshold
L0 pattern signal surfaces common attributes of L0 accounts rather than just counting them
assertions.rubric: PASS: Output attempts to identify what L0 accounts have in common — segment, onboarding path, product tier, or other deal attribute. FAIL: Output only reports the count/percentage without any pattern hypothesis.

assertions.text: Output applies G5: rubric levels described as analytical inputs with CS owning the response
